title: petamenes quadrangularis
intro: Petamenes quadrangularis, also known as Bael or Wood Apple, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiarrheal effects. The health benefits of Petamenes quadrangularis include the relief of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as the treatment of fever and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Petamenes quadrangularis include its bark, leaves, and fruit, which can be used to make teas, decoctions, and powders for various medicinal purposes.
